title: Modelmeter Events Snapshot
description: The changelog of record for the model layer: source-verified events covering what changed at AI providers — deprecations, price changes, ID renames, context/rate-limit changes, releases — plus the market events (funding, partnerships, legal, regulatory) that give them context. Records live or die on trust: every event carries sources, a verification status, and an explicit correction trail.

id.description: Stable slug. Convention: YYYY-MM-{primary-provider}-{short-description}. Never reused; corrections reference it via `corrects`.

announced_at.description: ISO date the change became public — the announcement, publication, filing, or occurrence date. For ongoing situations, the date the situation began.

effective_at.description: ISO date the change takes effect, when it differs from the announcement: a deprecation's sunset date, a price change's effective date. Absent when immediate or not applicable. `effective_at - today` is the consumer's runway.

detected_at.description: UTC timestamp Modelmeter's pipeline first detected the change. Absent on manually curated or historically backfilled entries.

type.description: Closed enum. Operational types (what changed in the model layer): pricing_change, model_launch, model_deprecation, model_unavailable, id_rename, model_swap, context_change, rate_limit_change, endpoint_change, capability_change, policy_change, correction. Market-context types: partnership, regulatory_action, acquisition, funding, open_source_release, legal_outcome, infrastructure, leadership_change.

severity.description: What the event demands of a consumer of the affected models. `breaking`: removes or renames something in use — model going away, ID changing, endpoint removed. `action_required`: changes the terms of continued use — price, rate limits, context window, behavior-relevant version bump. `informational`: adds or records something without demanding action — releases, funding, partnerships.

status.description: `unverified`: drafted by the pipeline, awaiting human review — consume with that caveat. `verified`: a human confirmed headline, dates, and sources. `corrected`: superseded — a `correction` event points here via `corrects`; kept for the audit trail, excluded from default API responses.

providers.description: Provider IDs affected. Includes providers we do not track pricing for (Meta, Microsoft, etc.) — events can involve more entities than the pricing dataset.

models.description: Affected model IDs in `provider/model` form, matching pricing/current.json where possible. Empty if event is provider-wide or industry-wide.

migration_target.description: For deprecations/renames/swaps: the provider-recommended replacement model ID in `provider/model` form. Absent if the provider names none.

corrects.description: For `type: correction` events only: the `id` of the event this correction supersedes. The superseded event's status becomes `corrected`. Corrections are never silent.

summary.description: Short paragraph (1-4 sentences) giving the relevant context. Agent-readable: lead with what changed and what a consumer must do. Avoid speculation; cite the source.

url.description: Primary source. Provider announcements, changelogs, regulatory filings, and reputable journalism only — no aggregator links.

quote.description: Verbatim excerpt from the source supporting the claim. Expected on all pipeline-detected operational events; providers edit their own changelogs, so the quote preserves what the source said at detection time.

archived_url.description: Archive snapshot (archive.org or similar) of the source at capture time.

sources.description: At least one source required. The first entry is the primary citation.

impact.description: Optional structured impact estimate. Free-form notes go in `summary`.
